en.extract:
The pied heron (Egretta picata), also known as the pied egret is a bird found in coastal and subcoastal areas of monsoonal northern Australia as well as some parts of Wallacea and New Guinea.


Taxonomy
The species was originally described by ornithologist John Gould in 1845.  Recent taxonomists put this species in the genus Egretta. There are no recognised subspecies.


Description
It is a small heron, 43–55 cm (17–22 in) long, with dark slaty wings, body, and crested head, with a white throat and neck. The appearance is similar to the white-necked heron. Males (247–280 g (8.7–9.9 oz)) are heavier than females (225–242 g (7.9–8.5 oz)), but the two are similar in appearance.
Immature birds lack the crest as well as the dark colouring on the head and may look like small versions of the white-necked heron. The juveniles were once classified as a separate species.


Distribution and habitat
Its habitat mainly comprises a range of wetlands and wet grasslands.


Behaviour


Call
The call of the pied heron is a loud 'awk' or 'ohrk' in flight. Soft cooing is given around the nest. Little else is known about vocalisations.


Breeding
Breeding takes place from February to May. It nests in trees above the water, including mangroves, often colonially with other species of heron. 1–2 blue-green eggs are laid in a shallow platform of sticks.


Feeding
It feeds on insects, frogs, crabs, fish and other small aquatic animals. Insects are the most important source of food. It may feed alone or in groups of up to a thousand individuals.


References


Bibliography

Marchant, S.; & Higgins, P.J. (Coordinators). (2000). Handbook of Australian, New Zealand and Antarctic Birds. Vol.1: Ratites to Ducks. Oxford University Press: Melbourne. ISBN 978-0-19-553068-1

The '''pied heron''' ('''''Egretta picata'''''), also known as the '''pied egret'''<ref name="pizzey">{{cite book |title=Field Guide to the Birds of Australia |last=Pizzey |first=Graham |author2=Knight, Frank |year=1997 |publisher=[[HarperCollinsPublishers]] |location=Sydney, Australia |isbn=0-207-18013-X |page=111}}</ref> is a bird found in coastal and subcoastal areas of [[monsoon]]al northern Australia as well as some parts of [[Wallacea]] and [[New Guinea]].

==Taxonomy==
[[File:Pied Heron (Juvenile) - Fogg Dam - Middle Point - Northern Territory - Australia.jpg|thumb|Pied heron (juvenile) - Fogg Dam - Middle Point - Northern Territory - Australia]]The [[species]] was originally described by [[ornithologist]] [[John Gould]] in 1845.  Recent [[taxonomist]]s put this species in the genus ''[[Egretta]]''. There are no recognised [[subspecies]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.damisela.com/zoo/ave/otros/ciconi/ardei/day/picata/taxae.htm|title=Pied Heron, ''Egretta picata'', Taxonomy|access-date=25 February 2010}}</ref>
[[File:Pied Heron feeding - Fogg Dam - Middle Point - Northern Territory - Australia.jpg|thumb|Pied heron feeding - Fogg Dam - Middle Point - Northern Territory - Australia]][[File:PiedHeronWalking2FoggDam260114.jpg|thumb|Pied heron in breeding plumage - Fogg Dam - Middle Point - Northern Territory - Australia]]

==Description==
It is a small [[heron]], {{cvt|43–55|cm}} long, with dark [[slaty]] wings, body, and crested head, with a white throat and neck. The appearance is similar to the [[white-necked heron]].<ref name="pizzey" /> Males ({{cvt|247–280|g}}) are heavier than females ({{cvt|225–242|g}}), but the two are similar in appearance.<ref name="kushlan" />

Immature birds lack the crest as well as the dark colouring on the head and may look like small versions of the [[white-necked heron]]. The juveniles were once classified as a separate species.<ref name="kushlan">{{cite book |title=The Herons |last=Kushlan |first=James Anthony |author2=Hancock, James |author3=Thelwell, David |year=2005 |publisher=[[Oxford University Press]] |isbn=0-19-854981-4 |page=170 }}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
Its habitat mainly comprises a range of [[wetland]]s and wet [[grassland]]s.

==Behaviour==

===Call===
The call of the pied heron is a loud 'awk' or 'ohrk' in flight.<ref name="pizzey"/> Soft cooing is given around the nest.<ref name="kushlan" /> Little else is known about vocalisations.<ref name="kushlan" />

===Breeding===
Breeding takes place from February to May.<ref name="pizzey"/> It nests in trees above the water, including [[mangrove]]s, often [[Bird colony|colonially]] with other species of heron. 1–2 blue-green eggs are laid in a shallow platform of sticks.<ref name="pizzey"/>

===Feeding===
It feeds on insects, frogs, crabs, fish and other small aquatic animals. Insects are the most important source of food.<ref name="kushlan" /> It may feed alone or in groups of up to a thousand individuals.<ref name="kushlan" />

zh.extract:
斑鹭（台湾作白頸黑鷺）（学名：Egretta picata）是一種分布於澳大利亞北部季風區的沿海和次沿海地區以及華萊士區和新幾內亞部分地區的鳥類。


分類
這個物種最初由鳥類學家約翰·古爾德於1845年描述。最近的分類學家將這個物種歸入白鷺屬（Egretta）屬。本種沒有被承認的亞種。


描述
這是一種小型鷺，長43-55公分，擁有深板岩色的翅膀、身體和冠狀頭部，喉嚨和頸部為白色。外觀類似於白頸鷺。 雄性（247-280公克）比雌性（225-242公克）重，但外觀相似。
亞成鳥類缺乏冠羽和頭部的深色，可能看起來像小型的白頸鷺。幼鳥曾被分類為一種單獨的物種。


分佈與棲地
其棲地主要包括各種濕地和濕草地。


行為


叫聲
白頸黑鷺的叫聲在飛行中為響亮的“awk”或“ohrk”。 在巢附近會發出柔和的咕咕聲。 其他聲音信息不多。


繁殖
繁殖期從二月至五月。 它在水面上的樹上築巢，包括紅樹林，通常與其他鷺類一起群居。巢由樹枝搭構成淺平台，產1-2枚藍綠色的蛋。


覓食
其食物包括昆蟲、青蛙、螃蟹、魚和其他小型水生動物。昆蟲是最重要的食物來源。 它可以單獨或成群覓食，群體可多達一千隻個體。


參考

BirdLife International (2006) Species factsheet: Ardea picata. Downloaded from http://www.birdlife.org（页面存档备份，存于互联网档案馆） on 14/11/2006
Marchant, S.; & Higgins, P.J. (Coordinators). (2000). Handbook of Australian, New Zealand and Antarctic Birds. Vol.1: Ratites to Ducks. Oxford University Press: Melbourne. ISBN 978-0-19-553068-1
